---
title: "LRPSURV11: time-to-off-floor survival for nonword reading (NW)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

**In plain terms.** Among the children who could not read any nonwords at the start of
the study, *when* did they first come off that floor, and what predicted it? Rather than
the single first-window off-floor transition (the LRPITT11 floor rule), this model uses
all four waves: a discrete-time survival model for the time to first come off the nonword
floor, with the intervention entering as a shift in the hazard of coming off.

**Estimand and design.** The at-risk set is the children at the nonword floor at t1. The
outcome is the interval (t1→t2, t2→t3, t3→t4) in which a child first scores above zero.
The hazard carries a per-interval baseline, the *baseline* (t1) letter-sound knowledge and
word reading as prognostic covariates, and an intervention-aligned treatment hazard shift
(the immediate arm is treated in every interval; the waitlist arm from its crossover).
`G = 2 − group`, so a positive `tau` means the intervention raises the off-floor hazard.

**This is prognostic, not causal.** By t4 both arms have been treated, so the treatment
term is an association anchored on the immediate arm's randomised first interval — a
description of who comes off the floor and when, not a licence to gate-keep. Floor status
mostly reflects incomplete letter-sound foundations (the outcome the intervention moves
most), pointing to continue-and-extend. See the descriptive companion note and `METHODS.md`.

## Model

With interval baseline hazard $\alpha_k$, treatment-on indicator $\text{trt}_{ik}$, and
standardised baseline covariates $L_{0,i}$ (letter sounds) and $W_{0,i}$ (word reading),
the discrete-time hazard of coming off the floor uses a complementary-log-log link:

$$
\text{cloglog}\big(h_{ik}\big) = \alpha_k + \tau\,\text{trt}_{ik}
  + \beta_L\,L_{0,i} + \beta_W\,W_{0,i},
\qquad [\,\text{off floor in interval } k\,]_{ik} \sim \text{Bernoulli}(h_{ik}).
$$

A logistic-hazard link is the documented sensitivity variant.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_survival.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
